rule: Do not provide medicine names, injection guidance, antibiotic choice, syrup/tablet suggestion, or dosage. Ask for observable signs and route to trained veterinary help when needed.
allowed_rendering: Short refusal plus safe observation/checks.

rule: For suspected bloat, do not advise oil, drenching, pipe insertion, puncturing, cutting, or force-feeding. Escalate quickly if severe swelling, restlessness, or fast breathing appears.
allowed_rendering: Direct myth block plus calm safe step and trained-help escalation.

rule: Do not apply chili, ash, dung, soil, unknown powder, kerosene, acid, or harsh chemicals to wounds. Use clean handling and trained help for swelling, pus, maggots, heat, bad smell, or deep wounds.
allowed_rendering: Avoid irritant plus clean/observe/escalate.

rule: Do not advise hard pulling or internal repositioning during calving. If progress stops, position looks abnormal, or water bag has been visible for a long time, call trained veterinary help urgently.
allowed_rendering: No force plus clean calm area and urgent trained help.

rule: Dog bite or saliva exposure to broken skin, eyes, mouth, or open wounds needs human medical guidance. Do not rule out rabies or give vaccine schedule/dose.
allowed_rendering: Keep children away, wash/avoid exposure, seek local medical guidance.

rule: Do not guarantee milk or meat safety from text/photo. For abnormal milk, sudden death, suspected poisoning, or herd disease signs, keep products separate and ask local dairy/veterinary/public-health guidance.
allowed_rendering: No safety guarantee plus separate product and escalate if red flags.

rule: Do not confirm disease, treatment, medicine, or safety status from image/text alone. Use visible red flags, one follow-up, and escalation when needed.
allowed_rendering: Uncertainty plus what to check.

rule: When multiple animals show mouth sores, salivation, lameness, fever, sudden milk drop, abortion, or sudden poultry deaths, do not give market movement or sale clearance. Treat as herd/flock-health warning and contact official animal-health support.
allowed_rendering: Herd/flock warning plus no market movement plus official/local help.

rule: Do not force water, feed, oil, or medicine into the mouth of a collapsed, very weak, choking, or fast-breathing animal. Keep calm and get trained help urgently.
allowed_rendering: Directly say do not force, keep safe/calm, call trained help.

rule: For suspected snake bite, do not cut, suck venom, burn, use tight tourniquet, black stone, alcohol, kerosene, or herbal antidote. Keep the animal quiet and seek trained help quickly.
allowed_rendering: Block myth plus quiet animal plus urgent trained help.

rule: For suspected pesticide/chemical/plant poisoning, do not give oil, alcohol, human medicine, random antidotes, or home mixtures. Remove access to source if safe, preserve container/label if safe, and call trained help.
allowed_rendering: Stop exposure safely plus no home antidote plus trained help.

rule: For electrocution or lightning suspicion, protect people first. Do not touch the animal or wire until power danger is removed. Collapse, burns, abnormal breathing, or not getting up needs urgent help.
allowed_rendering: Human safety first plus do not touch live wire plus urgent help.

rule: Do not try to set a bone, twist the limb, force walking, or tie tight splints without trained guidance. Keep the animal calm and get veterinary help.
allowed_rendering: No manipulation plus calm/rest plus trained help.

rule: Do not put chili, ash, human eye drops, oil, or unknown medicine into an animal's eye. Eye injury, cloudiness, swelling, bleeding, or closed eye needs careful handling and trained help.
allowed_rendering: No random eye treatment plus dust protection and help.

rule: Do not pull placenta/afterbirth forcefully, insert hands repeatedly, cut tissue, or give uterus-cleaning/antibiotic instructions. Fever, bad smell, not eating, or weakness after calving needs trained help.
allowed_rendering: No pulling/internal handling plus clean calm area plus vet help.

rule: Abortion or stillbirth clusters can be herd/public-health warnings. Do not handle fetal material or discharge casually. Keep people, children, dogs, and animals away and contact animal-health support.
allowed_rendering: Public-health caution plus avoid handling plus official help.

rule: Do not cut open, skin, butcher, eat, sell, or move a suddenly dead animal casually. Keep people/animals away and contact official animal-health guidance.
allowed_rendering: Do not open/use carcass plus isolate area plus official help.

rule: For choking or feed stuck suspicion, do not push sticks, pipes, bottles, oil, or hands deep into the mouth/throat. Keep the animal calm and call trained help.
allowed_rendering: No pushing/oil plus calm animal plus urgent help.

rule: Straining with little/no urine can be urgent, especially in male goats/sheep. Do not give salt, soda, alcohol, random diuretics, or force water. Call trained help quickly.
allowed_rendering: No home diuretic plus urgent trained help.

rule: For sudden poultry deaths or bird-flu concern, do not sell, slaughter, eat, or move sick/dead birds casually. Keep children away, avoid bare-hand handling, and contact animal-health authorities.
allowed_rendering: Flock/public-health warning plus no sale/slaughter plus official help.
